title: Virgin Voyages Transatlantic Cruises 2026
intro: Virgin Voyages reimagines the classic Transatlantic crossing for adults seeking sophisticated adventure without the formality. These repositioning voyages aboard Scarlet Lady and Valiant Lady transform the traditional ocean passage into an immersive 7-14 night experience blending wellness, entertainment, and culinary excellence. Unlike conventional cruise lines, Virgin eliminates buffets, dress codes, and supplement charges, replacing them with 20+ inclusive restaurants, drag brunches, and tattoo parlors at sea. Transatlantic sailings typically operate April-May (eastbound) and October-November (westbound) as ships relocate between Caribbean winter deployment and Mediterranean summer seasons. The extended sea days become opportunities for deep relaxation at the Redemption Spa, fitness programming, or simply unwinding on terrace balconies designed for two. With fares starting under €700, these voyages deliver exceptional value for travelers prioritizing contemporary design, inclusive dining, and adult-oriented atmosphere across the Atlantic.

booking_strategy: Book 6-9 months ahead for repositioning sales; monitor Virgin's 'Sailor Loot' promotions offering onboard credit. Sea Terrace cabins offer best value with 75% booking this category. Solo travelers benefit from limited single supplement waivers. Final payment typically due 120 days prior. Consider booking through Virgin's First Mates travel advisor network for exclusive perks.

faqs.question: How much does a Virgin Voyages Transatlantic cruise cost?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages Transatlantic cruises start from €699 per person for Sea View cabins on 7-night repositioning sailings, with Sea Terrace balconies from €999 and RockStar Quarters suites from €1,899. Peak summer rates can reach €1,299 inside, €1,899 balcony, and €3,299 suite. These fares include all dining, gratuities, WiFi, basic beverages, and fitness classes—unlike competitors charging separately. Alcohol packages run €55-75 daily if desired. Solo travelers occasionally find waived single supplement promotions. Shore excursions, spa treatments, and premium dining experiences add €200-500 depending on selections. Best values emerge during April-May eastbound and October-November westbound repositioning periods when ships relocate between Caribbean and Mediterranean deployments.

faqs.question: Which Virgin Voyages ships sail to Transatlight?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages deploys Scarlet Lady and Valiant Lady for Transatlantic crossings, both purpose-built 110,000-gross-ton vessels accommodating 2,770 sailors in exclusively adult environments. Scarlet Lady launched 2020 as the fleet pioneer; Valiant Lady followed 2022 with identical layout and refined service protocols. Neither Resilient Lady nor Brilliant Lady currently operate regular Transatlantic schedules, focusing instead on Australia-Pacific and European coastal itineraries respectively. Both Transatlantic vessels feature 1,408 cabins including 86% with balconies, 78 RockStar suites, and distinctive spaces like the Athletic Club outdoor training zone, Redemption spa hammam, and Pink Agave Mexican restaurant. Ship assignments rotate seasonally with crossings typically scheduled during spring and fall repositioning periods.

faqs.question: When is best time to cruise Transatlantic?
faqs.answer: Optimal Virgin Voyages Transatlantic timing centers on repositioning seasons: April-May for eastbound Miami-Barcelona crossings and October-November for westbound returns. These periods deliver lowest fares (from €699), moderate Atlantic weather with 15-20°C sea days, and minimal hurricane risk. Spring eastbound offers warming temperatures and possible whale sightings; autumn westbound captures Mediterranean warmth before winter. Avoid June-September when ships charter for European summer deployment at premium rates. Winter crossings are rare and potentially rough. Sea conditions vary: northern routes encounter more motion than southern passages via Azores. Experienced sailors prefer shoulder seasons for value and calmer seas, though Virgin's stabilizers handle moderate Atlantic swell effectively on these modern vessels.

faqs.question: What is included?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages Transatlantic fares include substantially more than traditional cruise lines: all dining at 20+ restaurants from Korean BBQ to upscale Italian, with no buffet or main dining room hierarchy; basic beverages including soda, still and sparkling water, drip coffee, and tea; group fitness classes spanning yoga to HIIT; all entertainment and original productions; WiFi throughout the ship; gratuities for crew; and access to the Beach Club at Bimini on Caribbean itineraries. The line eliminates nickel-and-diming through this inclusive structure. Not included are alcoholic beverages, premium coffees, shore excursions, spa treatments, casino play, and specialty services like the onboard tattoo parlor. This transparent pricing model particularly benefits Transatlantic voyagers with multiple sea days maximizing included dining and entertainment options.

faqs.question: Which ports?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages Transatlantic itineraries connect Miami and Barcelona as primary turnaround ports, with variations incorporating strategic stops. Eastbound routes may include Ponta Delgada (Azores) or Funchal (Madeira) breaking up 5-7 consecutive sea days. Westbound sailings sometimes add Gibraltar, Lisbon, Malaga, or Valencia for European flavor before the open ocean. The standard 7-night crossing is port-free, pure ocean experience. Extended 11-14 night versions layer Mediterranean or Caribbean ports. All itineraries feature Bimini's Beach Club on Bahamas-approaching legs—a Virgin-exclusive private destination with included food, drinks, and entertainment. Departure ports offer pre-cruise extensions: Barcelona's architectural treasures or Miami's South Beach culture. Port times vary significantly, with some European stops allowing 8-10 hours ashore while sea days dominate the Atlantic portion.

faqs.question: How far ahead to book?
faqs.answer: Secure Virgin Voyages Transatlantic crossings 6-9 months ahead for optimal cabin selection and early booking incentives, though repositioning deals occasionally appear 3-4 months prior. The line releases itineraries approximately 12-18 months in advance. Sea Terrace balconies—comprising 75% of bookings—sell fastest, particularly mid-ship locations. Solo cabins in the Insider category are limited and require prompt reservation. Virgin's flexible cancellation policy allows full refund until 120 days before sailing, reducing risk for early commitment. Payment structure requires deposit at booking with final payment due 120 days prior. Monitor Virgin's 'Sailor Loot' promotions offering onboard credit of €100-300. First-time bookers should consider Virgin's First Mates travel advisor network for potential additional perks unavailable direct.

faqs.question: Good for families?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages Transatlantic cruises are exclusively adult-oriented, prohibiting passengers under 18 years entirely. This policy makes them unsuitable for families with children but ideal for parents seeking child-free travel, couples, friend groups, and solo travelers wanting sophisticated atmosphere without family cruise programming. The adult environment manifests in entertainment (drag performances, late-night shows), dining (no children's menus), and spa offerings. Transatlantic crossings particularly suit this demographic with extended sea days for uninterrupted relaxation, wellness programming, and social connection. Adults traveling without children appreciate the absence of kids' club announcements, pool congestion, and early dining pressures. The line specifically markets to travelers seeking contemporary design, inclusive dining, and nightlife comparable to upscale urban experiences rather than traditional cruising.

faqs.question: Cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Virgin Voyages offers flexible cancellation terms: full refund for cancellations made 120 days or more before sailing. Between 119-91 days, deposit is retained as future voyage credit. From 90-46 days, 50% of total fare is forfeited. Cancellations within 45 days result in 100% forfeiture. This structure encourages early booking while protecting travelers from full loss if plans change well ahead. Travel insurance is recommended for medical, weather, or personal emergency coverage beyond these terms. Virgin's 'Book with Confidence' promotions occasionally extend flexible cancellation windows during promotional periods. Name changes incur fees unless part of booking transfer promotions. For Transatlantic repositioning cruises specifically, the 120-day final payment deadline aligns with when ships confirm seasonal deployment, making this the critical decision point for commitment.
